name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 51

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written (Ground level 64.30 m O D)

units: metres; typed Wimpey Geotech rotary-core log, descriptions transcribed verbatim. depth_m = base of interval below ground level; thickness_m base-to-base. Reduced Level (O D Level m O D) column used only to cross-check.

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL
Loose to medium dense orange brown and dark brown silty fine and medium SAND with rootlets
Medium dense orange brown slightly clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with some fine and medium angular fragments of sandstone
Orange brown fine grained SANDSTONE
annotations: cable percussion to 2.60m; borehole advanced by chiselling from 2.00m to 2.40m (1h) per Remarks
S>50 (75mm penetration) at 2.40m and Dj at 2.60m = rockhead; water column DRY at 2.60m

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately weathered thinly laminated slightly fissile highly fractured muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak. Orange ironstained subvertical and subhorizontal fractures
annotations: rotary core drilling starts 2.60m; dated 6/4 at 2.90m
orange ironstained fractures (with iron/water)

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded laminated SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Many thin carbonaceous laminae. Occasional yellow discoloured patches adjacent to major fractures. Bed of highly weathered thinly laminated fissile siltstone from 3.25m to 3.40m
annotations: thin carbonaceous laminae throughout
internal bed of highly weathered fissile siltstone 3.25m-3.40m

intervals.verbatim: Yellow fine becoming medium to coarse grained slightly weathered bedded SANDSTONE - strong. Some traces and thin laminae of coal. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Bed of orange completely weathered sandstone from 4.70m to 4.80m
annotations: coal traces and thin laminae (unnamed)
bed of orange completely weathered sandstone 4.70m-4.80m
OD anomaly: log O D 59.60 at 4.80m; arithmetic 64.30-4.80 = 59.50 (0.10 clerk slip); neighbouring O D values are self-consistent so depth 4.80m is retained

intervals.verbatim: Yellow, occasionally grey, fine and medium grained slightly weathered bedded faintly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Occasionally moderately strong. Many traces and thin wavy laminae and thin discontinuous lenses of coal up to 5mm thick. Closely and medium spaced, dipping 20 degrees, bedding plane fractures. Occasional 70 degrees to 80 degrees fractures. Completely weathered clayey bed from 6.65m to 6.75m
annotations: thin discontinuous coal lenses up to 5mm (unnamed)
completely weathered clayey bed 6.65m-6.75m
GWe (groundwater encountered) at 7.80m per casing note and Remarks
'See next sheet' at 7.90m; base at 7.90m (O D 56.40)

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong, locally moderately strong. Medium, occasionally closely spaced subhorizontal to 15 degrees bedding plane fractures. Dark reddish brown stain and pervasive yellow discolouration associated with occasional subvertical fractures from 11.80m to 12.00m and 12.50m to 13.60m. Carbonaceous laminae are convolute and subhorizontal to 15 degrees in places; thin dark grey silty laminae are cross bedded elsewhere with foresets dipping 20 degrees to 30 degrees. Beds of brown completely discoloured moderately weak sandstone from 12.45m to 12.70m
annotations: cross-bedded silty laminae, foresets dipping 20-30 degrees
discoloured/weakened zones 11.80-12.00m, 12.45-12.70m, 12.50-13.60m
starts page 2 (Sheet 2 of 4)

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine and fine to medium grained slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong, locally moderately weak to moderately strong. Many thin (<5mm) lenses of coal. Medium, occasionally closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Major subvertical orange pervasively discoloured fractures with associated weakening of adjacent rockmass from 16.30m to 16.60m
modifiers: locally moderately weak to moderately strong
annotations: many thin (<5mm) coal lenses (unnamed)
major discoloured/weakened fracture zone 16.30m-16.60m

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded CONGLOMERATE - strong. Generally elongate subangular to subrounded gravel sized clasts of siltstone set in a fine grained sandstone matrix
annotations: original names CONGLOMERATE: gravel-sized siltstone clasts in a fine grained sandstone matrix; classed sandstone (no conglomerate class in schema; matrix-defined)

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained slightly to moderately weathered bedded muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Many irregular subhorizontal and subvertical fractures. Many carbonaceous fossil wood fragments
annotations: many carbonaceous fossil wood fragments
base at 18.60m (O D 45.70) is the zigzag contact to the underlying grey carbonaceous siltstone described on Sheet 3

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated carbonaceous SILTSTONE - moderately strong and strong. Laminae vary from convolute to planar. Closely and medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures
annotations: top at 18.60m shown as 'See next sheet' on Sheet 2; described at top of Sheet 3
base at 20.90m (O D 43.40)

intervals.verbatim: White fine grained fresh, locally slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated SANDSTONE - strong. Medium, occasionally closely spaced 10 degrees to 20 degrees bedding plane fractures. Some subvertical fractures from 21.80m to 22.40m and 23.40m to 23.80m. Laminae are planar dipping 10 degrees to 20 degrees, occasionally convolute and subhorizontal. Beds of dark grey carbonaceous siltstone from 20.90m to 20.95m, 21.15m to 21.25m and 21.30m to 21.40m
annotations: thin dark grey carbonaceous siltstone beds 20.90-20.95m, 21.15-21.25m, 21.30-21.40m
core-run boundary at 23.90m within the bed
base at 24.30m (O D 40.00)

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated SILTSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Closely, occasionally very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures
annotations: core-run boundary at 26.30m within the bed

OD anomaly: log O D 36.50 at 27.00m; arithmetic 64.30-27.00 = 37.30 (0.80 mismatch). Depth column reads 27.00m clearly and is retained; had the O D 36.50 been correct the boundary would be 27.80m. Neighbouring O D (40.00@24.30, 35.65@28.65) are self-consistent, so the 36.50 value is the likely clerk slip

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated fissile silty carbonaceous MUDSTONE - moderately weak. Very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some bivalve fossils
annotations: some bivalve fossils (marine band indicator)
base at 28.65m (O D 35.65)

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured silty COAL - very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation

log draws coal top at 28.70m while the overlying mudstone base is at 28.65m: a 0.05m zigzag-contact zone. thickness_m taken base-to-base (28.65m->29.10m) for column continuity; verbatim coal-top depth is 28.70m
base at 29.10m (O D 35.20)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded slightly fissile carbonaceous muddy SILTSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some clasts and thin lenses of pale grey sandstone
annotations: clasts and thin lenses of pale grey sandstone
continues to Sheet 4 as 'See previous sheet'; base at 30.25m (O D 34.05)

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured thinly laminated fissile silty COAL - weak and very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation
base at 30.80m (O D 33.50)

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated slightly fissile sandy SILTSTONE - moderately strong. Laminae are convolute
annotations: core-run boundary line at 31.10m within the bed (legend and O D column continuous; not a lithological change)
base at 31.95m (O D 32.35)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated fissile silty MUDSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong
annotations: base at 32.50m (O D 31.80)

intervals.verbatim: Black thinly laminated fissile COAL - very weak
annotations: seam unnamed in original; no pencil correlation
thin 0.05m coal (32.50m-32.55m); base at 32.55m (O D 31.75)

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded thinly laminated silty SANDSTONE - strong. Many carbonaceous traces
annotations: many carbonaceous traces
last logged bed; END OF BOREHOLE at 32.90m (O D 31.40)

notes: Wimpey Geotech borehole record, Borehole 51, A167 Durham Western Bypass (Fig. 56, Sheets 1-4 of 4; drawing no. 24A REV 2/88, S/26290). Cable percussion 0.00-2.60m then rotary core drilling (Dando 150; Boyles BBS20; 412 core barrel; mist flush) 2.60-32.90m. Vertical. Ground level 64.30 m O D. Commenced 28/3/90; core logged 6/4/90; drawn/checked AMcL 10/4/90 and 28/6/90; originator DJM. Remarks (Sheet 4): borehole advanced by chiselling from 2.00m to 2.40m (1h); ground-water encountered at 7.80m; full return of circulation mist during rotary drilling; backfilled with bentonite/cement grout. Sequence is Pennine (Coal Measures) sandstone-siltstone-mudstone cyclothems with four thin coals (17.35-18.10m, 28.70-29.10m, 30.25-30.80m, 32.50-32.55m), all left UNNAMED in the original and carrying NO pencil seam correlations - so no seam names could be assigned. Bivalve fossils in the mudstone at 27.00-28.65m suggest a marine/faunal band but it is not named. Two Reduced-Level (O D column) clerk slips found on cross-check and flagged in-interval: (a) 4.80m written 59.60 vs arithmetic 59.50; (b) 27.00m written 36.50 vs arithmetic 37.30 - depth columns retained as written in both cases. A 0.05m zigzag mudstone/coal contact zone at 28.65-28.70m (interval 18) is noted. Interval 11 is described CONGLOMERATE (siltstone clasts in sandstone matrix), classed sandstone for lack of a conglomerate class. Handwritten 'NZ24SE/204' at the top of each sheet is the BGS index reference, not log content. Final depth 32.90m matches the index depth (32.9m).
